name: 3 Day Bromo, Madakaripura & Ijen Overland from Surabaya to Bali
description: 3-day overland private tour from Surabaya to Bali: Mount Bromo sunrise (private 4WD jeep, Day 2), Madakaripura Waterfall canyon descent (Day 2), and Kawah Ijen Blue Fire crater (midnight hike, Day 3) — finishing directly into Bali via the Ketapang–Gilimanuk ferry. No return to Surabaya. Health screening coordinated for Ijen crater access under BBKSDA SE.1658/KSA.9/2024. 100% private. Licensed operator NIB 1102230032918.

item.name: Day 1 — Surabaya to the Bromo Side for Overnight Staging
item.description: Private transfer from Surabaya to the Bromo staging area (Probolinggo / Cemoro Lawang). Check-in at Joglo Kecombrang Bromo. Early rest in preparation for the 02:00 Bromo departure.

item.name: Day 2 — Bromo Sunrise, Madakaripura, and Transfer Toward Ijen
item.description: 02:00 departure to Penanjakan viewpoint. Bromo stargazing and sunrise over the Tengger caldera. Private 4WD jeep to the crater base. Overland to Madakaripura Waterfall canyon descent (Indonesia's tallest waterfall). Dinner and private transfer east to the Bondowoso / Ijen side. Ijen health screening coordinated on arrival (SpO2, blood pressure, heart rate — BBKSDA SE.1658/KSA.9/2024). Check-in at Riverside Homestay.

item.name: Day 3 — Ijen Blue Fire Crater and Forward Finish into Bali
item.description: Midnight departure to Paltuding base. Guided ascent to Kawah Ijen crater rim (2,386 m). Blue Fire observation in the pre-dawn window. Gas masks and trekking poles provided. Descent, breakfast, then private transfer to Ketapang port. Ferry crossing Ketapang (Java) → Gilimanuk (Bali). Drop-off at agreed Bali location. Tour ends in Bali — no return to Surabaya.

subjectOf.name: Mount Bromo
subjectOf.description: Active volcano in the Tengger Semeru National Park. Penanjakan sunrise viewpoint and crater base accessed by private 4WD jeep. Elevation 2,329 m.

subjectOf.name: Madakaripura Waterfall
subjectOf.description: Indonesia's tallest waterfall (estimated 200 m). Canyon wade through narrow gorge passages. Located in Bromo Tengger Semeru National Park.

subjectOf.name: Kawah Ijen
subjectOf.description: Active stratovolcano with the world's largest acidic crater lake and the world's largest visible blue fire phenomenon. Elevation 2,386 m.

offers.description: Per-person price by group size: IDR 6,300,000 (1 pax) · IDR 3,570,000 (2) · IDR 3,275,000 (3) · IDR 3,050,000 (4–5) · IDR 2,850,000 (6–7) · IDR 2,550,000 (8–10) · IDR 2,450,000 (11+). All-inclusive: private transport Surabaya to Bali, dedicated crew, entrance fees, 4WD jeep (Bromo), gas masks + trekking poles (Ijen), health screening coordination, Ketapang–Gilimanuk ferry, mineral water, breakfast daily, T-shirt.

mainEntity.name: What is included in the 3 Day Bromo, Madakaripura & Ijen Overland price?

acceptedAnswer.text: All JVTO packages include: private transport (AC MPV for 1–3 guests, Toyota Hiace for 4–9), dedicated crew, all entrance fees and permits, mineral water daily, pick-up to drop-off assistance, and a JVTO travel T-shirt. This overland tour additionally includes a private 4WD jeep for Mount Bromo, gas masks and trekking poles for Ijen, health-screening coordination (BBKSDA regulations), the Ketapang–Gilimanuk ferry crossing into Bali, and breakfast on Days 2 and 3. International flights, travel insurance, and personal expenses are not included.

mainEntity.name: Does this tour end in Bali or return to Surabaya?

acceptedAnswer.text: This tour ends in Bali. After the Ijen hike on Day 3, JVTO transfers you to Ketapang port in Banyuwangi for the ferry crossing to Gilimanuk, then continues to your agreed Bali drop-off point. There is no return to Surabaya. If you need a Surabaya-end itinerary covering the same three sites, see the 3 Day Ijen, Bromo & Madakaripura Waterfall Discovery from Surabaya.

mainEntity.name: When does the Ijen health screening happen on this tour?

acceptedAnswer.text: On this tour, health screening is coordinated on the evening of Day 2 — after Madakaripura Waterfall and on arrival at the Bondowoso hotel, before the midnight Ijen departure on Day 3. As required under BBKSDA access regulations (Surat Edaran SE.1658/KSA.9/2024), licensed medical staff measure SpO2, blood pressure, heart rate, and respiratory history. The resulting QR-coded certificate is checked by BBKSDA at the crater access gate. No valid QR code means no access to the crater zone.

mainEntity.name: What happens if I do not pass the Ijen health screening?

acceptedAnswer.text: If screening staff or the JVTO team advise that you should not attempt the hike, JVTO follows that decision — it protects your safety and others on the trail. The climb is cancelled for that individual (not the group); alternatives include waiting at base camp or a safe zone. The screening session cost is non-refundable. The group continues.

mainEntity.name: What happens if Bromo or Ijen is closed on my tour dates?

acceptedAnswer.text: JVTO provides a written Plan B briefed before departure, not improvised at the gate. If a site closes due to volcanic activity or park authority order, an alternative route is offered. Guests may rebook at no penalty if the primary sites are inaccessible on the booked dates due to official closure. JVTO monitors MAGMA Indonesia / PVMBG and BBKSDA advisories daily and contacts guests proactively if closures are imminent.
